title: Pachystigma obovata
taxonomy: Pachystigma obovata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Pachystigma. The plant is a member of the tribe Millettieae. Further classification includes it being part of the subtribe Millettinae. This classification is based on its characteristics and relationships to other plants in the Fabaceae family.
uses: Pachystigma obovata has been traditionally used in West African medicine to treat various ailments. Its leaves and roots are believed to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating skin conditions and wounds. The plant is also used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, it is used to treat gastrointestinal problems such as diarrhea and constipation. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Pachystigma obovata.
